title: Independent oracle discipline: fixtures hand-written from spec, not generated from code under test
description: Every fixture file and every test doc-comment names the spec section it derives from (e.g. 'RFC 8620 §5.1'). The README.md explains why: serializing then deserializing with the same code proves only internal consistency, not spec conformance. A contributor may suggest generating fixtures from the types to save time — that would destroy the test suite's ability to catch systematic spec misreadings. The only valid oracle is a document written from spec text before the Rust code exists.
